breakfast.name: Poha with Peanuts
breakfast.description: Flattened rice flakes sautéed with mustard seeds, curry leaves, and peanuts.
breakfast.tip: Add a squeeze of lemon and fresh coriander for extra vitamins.

breakfast.name: Moong Dal Chilla
breakfast.description: Savory pancakes made from ground yellow moong lentils.
breakfast.tip: Add grated vegetables like carrots and spinach for extra nutrients.

breakfast.name: Oats Upma
breakfast.description: Rolled oats cooked with vegetables, mustard seeds, and spices.
breakfast.tip: Use steel-cut oats for lower glycemic index.

breakfast.name: Idli with Sambar
breakfast.description: Steamed rice-lentil cakes with lentil-vegetable soup.
breakfast.tip: The fermentation process increases bioavailability of nutrients.

breakfast.name: Egg Bhurji with Whole Wheat Toast
breakfast.description: Scrambled eggs with onions, tomatoes, green chilies, and spices.
breakfast.tip: Use minimum oil and include whole wheat toast for complex carbs.

breakfast.name: Sprouts Salad
breakfast.description: Mixed sprouted moong, chana, and methi with lemon and spices.
breakfast.tip: Sprouts are richest in nutrients in the morning; eat them fresh.

lunch.name: Dal Tadka with Brown Rice
lunch.description: Yellow lentils tempered with cumin, garlic, and ghee, with brown rice.
lunch.tip: Brown rice has more fiber and lower glycemic index than white rice.

lunch.name: Chicken Curry with Roti
lunch.description: Tender chicken in a spiced tomato-onion gravy with whole wheat flatbreads.
lunch.tip: Use skinless chicken and minimal oil to reduce saturated fat.

lunch.name: Rajma Chawal
lunch.description: Red kidney beans in a rich tomato gravy served with steamed rice.
lunch.tip: Rajma is a complete protein when paired with rice.

lunch.name: Palak Paneer with Roti
lunch.description: Cottage cheese cubes in a creamy spinach gravy.
lunch.tip: Add a squeeze of lemon to increase iron absorption from spinach.

lunch.name: Fish Curry with Rice
lunch.description: Rohu or Surmai fish in tangy coconut or tomato-based curry.
lunch.tip: Fish is an excellent source of omega-3 fatty acids and lean protein.

dinner.name: Khichdi with Ghee
dinner.description: Soft rice and lentil porridge, India's comfort food and ideal recovery meal.
dinner.tip: Light and easy to digest; perfect after evening workouts.

dinner.name: Grilled Chicken with Sabzi
dinner.description: Grilled spiced chicken with mixed vegetable side dish.
dinner.tip: Keep dinner protein-rich and low-carb for muscle recovery and fat loss.

dinner.name: Mixed Vegetable Dal
dinner.description: Mixed lentils cooked with seasonal vegetables and minimal spices.
dinner.tip: Add drumstick (moringa) for extra calcium and iron.

dinner.name: Paneer Bhurji with Roti
dinner.description: Crumbled cottage cheese with onions, tomatoes, and spices.
dinner.tip: Use low-fat paneer to reduce calorie density while keeping protein high.

snacks.name: Roasted Chana
snacks.description: Dry roasted chickpeas, a classic Indian snack.
snacks.tip: Keep a small pouch in your gym bag for a post-workout protein boost.

snacks.name: Greek Yogurt with Fruits
snacks.description: Hung curd / Greek-style yogurt with seasonal fruits.
snacks.tip: An excellent pre-workout snack; eat 60-90 minutes before exercise.

snacks.name: Banana & Peanut Butter
snacks.description: Classic combination of quick carbs and healthy fats.
snacks.tip: Perfect pre-workout snack 45-60 minutes before training.

snacks.name: Makhana (Fox Nuts)
snacks.description: Roasted fox nuts lightly seasoned with rock salt and pepper.
snacks.tip: Very low in calories and high in magnesium — great for muscle function.

general_tips: Drink at least 2.5–3 litres of water daily for active individuals.
Drink 500ml water 2 hours before a workout.
Sip 150–250ml water every 15–20 minutes during exercise.
Rehydrate with 500ml per 0.5kg body weight lost post-workout.
Coconut water is excellent for natural electrolyte replenishment.
Avoid sugary sports drinks; opt for nimbu pani (lemon water) with a pinch of salt.
Jeera (cumin) water aids digestion and hydration.
Herbal teas like tulsi or ginger count towards daily fluid intake.
hydration.pre_workout: 500ml water or coconut water 30–60 min before exercise
hydration.post_workout: 500ml water or nimbu pani immediately after exercise
